name_correction_note: Original draft addressed 'International Enforcement Team' generically — David M. Harrison confirmed as head of Mayer Brown International Enforcement & Asset Recovery practice. Addressed to him directly.

outreach_draft.subject: Gatekeeper Liability Recovery — Offshore Fraud Judgment — International Enforcement
outreach_draft.body:
Dear Mr. Harrison,

I am reaching out regarding an international fraud recovery matter that may align with Mayer Brown's international enforcement and asset recovery practice.

Our client holds an uncollected fraud judgment against an offshore foreign corporation (Chinese-registered entity). The defendant has no reachable assets. The recovery strategy pursues gatekeeper liability — auditors, financial institutions, and professional advisors positioned to prevent or identify the fraud — seeking settlement rather than extended litigation.

Mayer Brown's cross-border enforcement experience, your practice's track record recovering assets when debtors resist satisfaction through offshore relocation strategies, and your bilateral familiarity with auditor liability reflect strong structural alignment with this matter.

I would welcome a brief preliminary conversation. No commitment implied. Case details available upon request.

Sincerely,

Corporate X-Factor Operations
Judgment Monetization Group
corporatezfactor.com

optimizations_applied: Direct email found: dharrison@mayerbrown.com — David M. Harrison, head of International Enforcement & Asset Recovery (was: generic team contact page)
Attorney name corrected from 'Mayer Brown International Enforcement Team' to 'David M. Harrison'
Body tailored to Harrison's specific cross-border enforcement mandate
